headline: Agent deployment moved into production at scale while vector search and HNSW broke out as the retrieval substrate underneath it.
body: Agents dominated the quarter's new items, with Spotify running Claude agents across more than 20 million lines of code, Cursor shipping an iOS app driven by always-on cloud agents, and Google Cloud demonstrating production multi-agent systems built on ADK and the Model Context Protocol. Underneath the agent layer, vector search and HNSW broke out, and new entrants including model architecture, retry mechanisms, elastic compute, control plane, and error handling point to a shift toward the infrastructure plumbing that keeps agents running. Frontier model news clustered around pricing and availability, with GPT-5.6, Anthropic's Mythos, and cost pressure on labs surfacing billing transparency as a recurring theme. The top rotation movers, including scoped skills, AI-assisted development, and cache hit rate, all sit on the lagging side, indicating the engineering and orchestration topics are cooling relative to the breakouts.

cited_items.title: Mythos is Back, OpenAI Releases GPT 5.6, Apple’s Price Increases
How Spotify runs agents across 20M+ lines of code, with Niklas Gustavsson
Build a multi-agent system using ADK & MCP
